title: Handle a Full Bag in a Traditional Small Sort System
summary: Operator flow for a traditional small sort system when a destination bag becomes full: close the destination, print and apply a label, zip the bag, place it on the takeaway belt, and reopen the chute or destination for continued parcel flow.
when_to_use: Use when operating a traditional small sort system and a destination or chute bag becomes full and must be processed using the manual bagging sequence described in the source.
not_for: Do not use for OptiSweep AGV/tipper bag-out workflows.
Do not use as a troubleshooting procedure for label printer failures, chute reopening failures, or other exceptions not covered by the source.
Do not use for belted ACB or Fortna automated consolidated bagging flows shown only for comparison in the source.

responsibilities: Monitor assigned destinations or chutes in the traditional small sort system.
When a bag is full, perform the documented sequence: close destination, print label, apply label, zip bag, place bag on takeaway belt, and reopen chute or destination.

safety_notes: The source characterizes the traditional process as operator-intensive, slow, physically strenuous, and inefficient.
No source-supported lockout, guarding, ergonomic control, or production-stop instructions are provided in this packet.
access_or_tools_needed: Assigned destination or chute in a traditional small sort system

access_required: Operator access to assigned destination or chute area
Ability to print and apply a bag label
system_preconditions: A traditional small sort system is in operation.
The operator is assigned a section of destinations on the sorter.
A destination bag has become full or is observed to be full.

visual_references.description: Training slide/frame describing the traditional small sort bagging flow and operator-assigned destinations.

steps.title: Monitor the assigned destination or chute
steps.purpose: Identify when the destination bag has become full and the manual bagging sequence must begin.
steps.instruction: Monitor the assigned destination or chute in the traditional small sort system until the bag becomes full.
steps.expected_result: The operator identifies a full bag at the assigned destination or chute.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator is monitoring assigned destinations and recognizes when a bag is full.
steps.failure_condition: The source does not define how fullness is measured or what to do if bag status is unclear.

actions.label: Monitor assigned destination
actions.exact_action: Monitor the assigned destination or chute until the bag becomes full.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Traditional small sort comparison slide showing operators assigned about 20-25 destinations and the manual full-bag handling sequence.

stop_or_escalate_if: Bag fullness cannot be determined from the available operating cues.
The documented sequence cannot be started because the operating condition does not match the source-described traditional flow.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: In a traditional small sort system, operators are assigned sections of destinations (20-25) on a sorter; when a bag is full the operator closes the destination, prints and applies a label, zips the bag, places it on a takeaway belt, and reopens the chute.

steps.title: Close the destination
steps.purpose: Stop additional parcels from continuing into the full bag while it is being processed.
steps.instruction: Close the destination when the bag is full.
steps.expected_result: The destination is closed before the full bag is handled further.
steps.healthy_condition: The destination is closed and the operator can proceed with bag handling.
steps.failure_condition: The destination cannot be closed or the source does not provide exception handling for closure problems.

actions.label: Close destination
actions.exact_action: Close the destination when the bag is full.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Text on the traditional small sort slide stating that when a bag is full the operator closes the destination.

stop_or_escalate_if: The documented sequence cannot be completed as described in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When a bag is full the operator closes the destination, prints and applies a label, zips the bag, places it on a takeaway belt, and reopens the chute.

steps.title: Print the bag label
steps.purpose: Generate the label required for the full bag before it is moved onward.
steps.instruction: Print the bag label as described in the source.
steps.expected_result: A bag label is printed for the full bag.
steps.healthy_condition: The label prints and is available to apply to the bag.
steps.failure_condition: The label does not print; the source provides no troubleshooting steps for this condition.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Traditional flow text indicating the operator prints and applies a label after closing the destination.

stop_or_escalate_if: The label printing step cannot be completed using the documented operating flow.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When a bag is full the operator closes the destination, prints and applies a label, zips the bag, places it on a takeaway belt, and reopens the chute.

steps.title: Apply the printed label to the bag
steps.purpose: Identify the full bag before it is zipped and moved to the takeaway belt.
steps.instruction: Apply the printed label to the bag.
steps.expected_result: The printed label is attached to the full bag.
steps.healthy_condition: The bag has the printed label applied.
steps.failure_condition: The label cannot be applied or is unavailable because the prior print step failed; the source provides no exception handling.

actions.label: Apply label
actions.exact_action: Apply the printed label to the bag.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Traditional flow text indicating the operator prints and applies a label.

stop_or_escalate_if: The printed label is missing or unusable.
The bag cannot be labeled as described in the source.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When a bag is full the operator closes the destination, prints and applies a label, zips the bag, places it on a takeaway belt, and reopens the chute.

steps.title: Zip up the bag
steps.purpose: Secure the full bag before moving it to the takeaway belt.
steps.healthy_condition: The bag is zipped and ready to be moved.
steps.failure_condition: The bag cannot be zipped or the source provides no guidance for bag closure issues.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Traditional flow text indicating the operator zips the bag after labeling.

stop_or_escalate_if: The sequence cannot continue safely or as documented.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When a bag is full the operator closes the destination, prints and applies a label, zips the bag, places it on a takeaway belt, and reopens the chute.

steps.title: Place the bag on the takeaway belt
steps.purpose: Move the completed full bag out of the destination handling position.
steps.instruction: Place the bag on the takeaway belt.
steps.expected_result: The full, labeled, zipped bag is placed on the takeaway belt.
steps.healthy_condition: The bag has been moved onto the takeaway belt.
steps.failure_condition: The bag cannot be placed on the takeaway belt or the source provides no alternate handling path.

actions.label: Move bag to takeaway belt
actions.exact_action: Place the bag on the takeaway belt.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Traditional flow text indicating the operator places the bag on a takeaway belt.

stop_or_escalate_if: The takeaway belt is unavailable or the bag cannot be placed on it.
The documented sequence cannot be completed.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When a bag is full the operator closes the destination, prints and applies a label, zips the bag, places it on a takeaway belt, and reopens the chute.

steps.title: Reopen the chute or destination
steps.purpose: Return the destination to service so additional parcels can continue to fall into a new bag.
steps.instruction: Reopen the chute or destination so additional parcels can continue to fall into a new bag.
steps.expected_result: The chute or destination is reopened for continued parcel flow.
steps.healthy_condition: The destination is reopened and available for continued operation.
steps.failure_condition: The chute or destination cannot be reopened; the source provides no troubleshooting steps for this condition.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Traditional flow text indicating the operator reopens the chute after placing the bag on the takeaway belt.

stop_or_escalate_if: The chute or destination cannot be reopened.
The operating sequence cannot be restored to continued parcel flow.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: When a bag is full the operator closes the destination, prints and applies a label, zips the bag, places it on a takeaway belt, and reopens the chute.

success_criteria: The full bag is closed, labeled, zipped, and placed on the takeaway belt.
The chute or destination is reopened for continued parcel flow.
The documented manual sequence is completed in the order described by the source.
healthy_conditions: Operators are assigned destination sections and can monitor them for full bags.
A full bag can be processed by closing the destination, labeling, zipping, moving it to the takeaway belt, and reopening the chute.
Parcel flow can continue after the chute or destination is reopened.
failure_conditions: The bag cannot be labeled or zipped.
The bag cannot be placed on the takeaway belt.
The chute or destination cannot be reopened.
Any condition requiring troubleshooting beyond the linear operating flow described in the source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the documented sequence cannot be completed because the source only describes the operating flow.
Escalate if the label does not print because the source does not provide troubleshooting steps.
Escalate if the destination or chute cannot be reopened because the source does not provide troubleshooting or recovery guidance.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Traditional small sort bagging comparison slide showing the full-bag handling sequence.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: In a traditional small sort system, operators are assigned sections of destinations (20-25) on a sorter; when a bag is full the operator closes the destination, prints and applies a label, zips the bag, places it on a takeaway belt, and reopens the chute.

missing_details: The source does not define the exact method or control used to close the destination.
The source does not define the exact method used to print the bag label.
The source does not define label placement requirements on the bag.
The source does not define how a new bag is installed after the full bag is removed.
The source does not provide troubleshooting, recovery, or exception-handling steps for print failures or reopening failures.
The source does not provide time estimates, staffing variations, or explicit safety controls beyond describing the process as physically strenuous.
